things.name: Unirii Square (Piata Unirii)
things.description: Cluj's magnificent main square dominated by the Gothic St. Michael's Church and the equestrian statue of Matthias Corvinus; the vibrant heart of the city

things.name: St. Michael's Church
things.description: Romania's second-largest Gothic church from the 14th century with a soaring 76-metre neo-Gothic tower rebuilt after the 1763 earthquake; the city's landmark

things.name: Botanical Garden
things.description: One of Romania's finest botanical gardens with 14,000 plant species from around the world; the Japanese garden, rose garden and greenhouse collections are outstanding

things.name: Cluj National History Museum
things.description: Major museum in the beautiful Baroque Banffy Palace with extensive collections on the history of Transylvania from prehistoric times to the modern era

things.name: Romanian National Opera Cluj
things.description: One of Romania's leading opera companies performing in an imposing neo-Baroque building; tickets are extremely affordable by Western European standards

things.name: Turda Salt Mine
things.description: Extraordinary 2,000-year-old salt mine 30km from Cluj with a spectacular underground lake, rowing boats, Ferris wheel and concerts held 120 metres underground

things.name: Central Park
things.description: Victorian-era city park with a large lake, casino building, boating and the summer stage of the National Theatre; a beloved green lung in the city centre

things.name: Transylvanian Food and Culture
things.description: Cluj-Napoca's thriving restaurant scene celebrates Transylvanian cuisine: ciorba de burta, paprikash and palinca brandy in atmospheric historic buildings

things.name: Banffy Castle (Bontida)
things.description: Ruined Baroque palace 20km north of Cluj nicknamed the Versailles of Transylvania; being restored and hosting the annual Electric Castle music festival

things.name: Cluj Arena
things.description: Modern 30,200-seat stadium home of CFR Cluj, Romania's dominant club with multiple league titles and Europa League appearances including group stages
